findings.description: Signature graphic is repositioned and scaled differently in the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 1 of the PDF, the handwritten signature appears inline to the right of the sender block. In the HTML, the signature is moved below the sender information and appears smaller, altering its placement relative to the memo header content.

findings.description: Header typography and alignment are simplified in the HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses serif styling with underlined 'MEMORANDUM' and more formal spacing/alignment for the memo fields, while the HTML uses sans-serif text and a horizontal rule under the title. Headings remain distinct, but the original visual emphasis is not closely preserved.

findings.description: List formatting is visually weakened in the outline section.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows the outline items with clearer Roman numeral alignment and indentation. In the HTML, the items are still separated line by line but appear more like plain text with reduced structured indentation.

findings.description: Section heading styling differs from the PDF.
findings.evidence: On pages 2 through 5, the PDF uses serif section heads with numbered roman numerals and strong typographic distinction. The HTML renders these headings in sans-serif bold with a different visual hierarchy. Meaning is preserved, but styling is not closely matched.

findings.description: Bullet indentation and text emphasis are slightly altered.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF bullet list has tighter indentation and serif text; the HTML preserves bullets but uses different spacing and wrapping, making the list appear looser though still readable and structurally intact.

findings.description: Bullet list formatting is slightly changed in the oral comments summary.
findings.evidence: On page 3, the PDF presents bold-underlined topic labels within bullets. The HTML preserves the bullets and bold labels, but spacing, wrap points, and typographic emphasis differ, reducing fidelity to the original formatting.

findings.description: Bullet list formatting is slightly changed in the written comments summary.
findings.evidence: On page 4, the PDF uses bold-underlined topic labels and compact list spacing. The HTML preserves the list content and order, but uses different line wrapping and a looser visual presentation.

findings.description: Attachment list numbering and indentation are less formally rendered.
findings.evidence: On page 5, the PDF shows a Roman-numeral attachment list with consistent hanging indentation. The HTML retains the items and numbering but renders them with simplified spacing and weaker indentation.
